segments.caption: The video opens with a blue title screen displaying 'C Cube Academy' and an Instagram call-to-action, transitioning into a starry night sky. The scene shifts to a rooftop at night where a young boy in a blue checkered shirt and dark pants is using binoculars to look at the sky. Another boy, wearing a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and glasses, approaches him. The boy in yellow questions him in French, asking what he's doing with the binoculars at night, with subtitles providing the English translation. The boy with the binoculars explains he's trying to identify planets in the solar system for an upcoming oral exam. The boy in yellow, now acting as a tutor, asks him to define the solar system. The boy responds confidently, describing it as the sun and the celestial bodies orbiting it, including planets like Earth. The boy in yellow praises his answer and asks how many planets are in the solar system, setting up the next part of the lesson. The rooftop setting features a wooden floor and a white railing, with a dark cityscape and stars visible in the background, creating a calm, educational atmosphere.

discovered_new_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers, holding green binoculars. He is initially focused on the sky, then turns to answer questions with a confident expression.
discovered_new_entities.Boy in Yellow Shirt: A boy with short brown hair, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and dark sneakers. He approaches the other boy, gestures while speaking, and acts as a tutor, asking questions about the solar system with a friendly and encouraging demeanor.
discovered_new_entities.Binoculars: Green handheld binoculars used by the boy in the blue checkered shirt to observe the night sky.
discovered_new_entities.Rooftop Setting: A wooden rooftop with a white railing, overlooking a dark cityscape under a starry night sky, serving as the backdrop for the educational interaction.

segments.caption: The video opens on a rooftop at night, where the Boy with Binoculars stands beside the Boy in Yellow Shirt, who gestures while asking about the number of planets in the solar system. The Boy with Binoculars confidently answers 'There are 8,' and the scene transitions to a starry space background displaying the eight planets in order, each labeled with its French name: Mercure, Vénus, Terre, Mars, Jupiter, Saturne, Uranus, and Neptune. Returning to the rooftop, the Boy in Yellow Shirt asks if the other boy knows the characteristics of each planet, to which the Boy with Binoculars admits he does not. The Boy in Yellow Shirt reassures him and invites him on a virtual journey to visit each planet. The Boy with Binoculars agrees enthusiastically, and the Boy in Yellow Shirt gestures toward a rocket that suddenly appears, launching with a fiery blast. He instructs the boy to get on the rocket, initiating their virtual space adventure. The rooftop setting remains consistent throughout, with a dark cityscape and starry sky, while the dialogue is supported by bilingual French-English subtitles.

active_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers, holding green binoculars. He begins with a confident expression, answers questions, shows uncertainty when asked about planetary characteristics, and then becomes excited at the prospect of a virtual journey.
active_entities.Boy in Yellow Shirt: A boy with short brown hair, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and dark sneakers. He acts as a tutor, gesturing while asking questions, showing encouragement, and inviting the other boy on a virtual journey. He remains calm and friendly throughout the interaction.
active_entities.Binoculars: Green handheld binoculars held by the Boy with Binoculars, used to observe the night sky at the beginning of the scene.
active_entities.Rooftop Setting: A wooden rooftop with a white railing, overlooking a dark cityscape under a starry night sky, serving as the consistent backdrop for the educational interaction.

discovered_new_entities.Rocket: A white and gray rocket with a blue nose cone and yellow flames, appearing suddenly on the rooftop and launching with a fiery blast, serving as the vehicle for the virtual journey.

segments.caption: The scene opens on a rooftop at night, where the Boy in Yellow Shirt gestures excitedly toward a suddenly appearing Rocket, inviting the Boy with Binoculars to board. The Boy with Binoculars, initially holding his green binoculars, expresses awe at the rocket, exclaiming its awesomeness. The Rocket then launches with a burst of yellow flames, carrying both boys into the starry expanse of space. As they soar through the cosmos, the Boy in Yellow Shirt explains their journey begins with the first planet of the solar system, Mercury. The Rocket approaches a large, cratered gray planet—Mercury—set against a vibrant nebula backdrop. The Boy in Yellow Shirt continues to narrate, stating Mercury is the closest and smallest planet to the Sun, with extreme temperature fluctuations between -173°C and 427°C, displayed via a red thermometer graphic. He adds that a year on Mercury lasts 88 days, shown with a calendar icon. Throughout, the boys remain seated on the Rocket, smiling and engaged, as the environment shifts from the urban rooftop to the vast, twinkling void of space.

active_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers, initially holding green binoculars. He expresses excitement upon seeing the rocket, then sits on the rocket with a joyful expression as they travel through space.
active_entities.Boy in Yellow Shirt: A boy with short brown hair, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and dark sneakers. He acts as a guide, gesturing and speaking throughout the journey, explaining Mercury’s characteristics with a calm and encouraging demeanor.
active_entities.Rocket: A white and gray rocket with a blue nose cone and yellow flames, which launches from the rooftop and carries the two boys through space. It remains a central vehicle, with the boys seated on top, moving smoothly through the cosmos.
active_entities.Rooftop Setting: A wooden rooftop with a white railing, overlooking a dark cityscape under a starry night sky, serving as the initial backdrop before the rocket launches and the scene transitions to space.
active_entities.Binoculars: Green handheld binoculars held by the Boy with Binoculars at the start, which he later puts down as he boards the rocket, no longer visible after the launch.

discovered_new_entities.Mercury: A large, gray, cratered planet depicted in space, shown against a colorful nebula. It is identified as the first planet of the solar system, with on-screen text and graphics highlighting its proximity to the Sun, small size, extreme temperature range (-173°C to 427°C), and 88-day year.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars seated on the Rocket, floating in space near Mercury, which is depicted as a large, gray, cratered planet against a vibrant nebula. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that a year on Mercury lasts 88 days, prompting the Boy with Binoculars to exclaim in amazement. The Rocket then propels forward, leaving Mercury behind as the boys transition to their next destination. The environment shifts dramatically to a fiery, orange-hued space dominated by the Sun, with Venus now appearing as a large, glowing, rocky planet. The Boy in Yellow Shirt informs the Boy with Binoculars that Venus is the second closest planet to the Sun and is often called Earth’s twin due to their similar size and composition. The Boy with Binoculars expresses surprise and curiosity, asking why, to which the Boy in Yellow Shirt replies that it’s because of their similarities in size and composition. The Boy with Binoculars then inquires if Venus is habitable, and the Boy in Yellow Shirt responds that scientists once believed it might have had liquid water on its surface, hinting at its potential for past habitability. Throughout, the Rocket continues to carry them smoothly through space, with the Sun’s intense glow illuminating the scene.

active_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers. He is seated on the Rocket, initially surprised and amazed by Mercury’s 88-day year, then curious and engaged as he learns about Venus, asking questions with expressive facial reactions.
active_entities.Boy in Yellow Shirt: A boy with short brown hair, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and dark sneakers. active_entities.Mercury: A large, gray, cratered planet shown against a colorful nebula, with on-screen text indicating '88 jours' (88 days) to highlight its short year. It is visible at the start of the segment before the Rocket moves on.

discovered_new_entities.Venus: A large, glowing, rocky planet with a fiery orange and yellow surface, shown in close proximity to the Sun. It is introduced as the second planet from the Sun and described as Earth’s twin due to similarities in size and composition.
discovered_new_entities.Sun: A massive, bright, orange-yellow star with visible solar flares and a glowing corona, dominating the background as the Rocket approaches Venus. It provides intense illumination and sets a warm, radiant atmosphere for the latter half of the segment.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars seated on the Rocket, now flying near the Sun, with Venus prominently displayed as a large, fiery orange planet in the foreground. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that scientists believe Venus once had liquid water on its surface, and then answers the Boy with Binoculars’ question about Venus’s orbital period, revealing it takes about 225 Earth days. He continues by stating Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system, with a temperature of 460°C, which is visually emphasized by a digital thermometer graphic. The Boy with Binoculars reacts with shock, exclaiming 'Oh my God!' at the extreme heat. The Boy in Yellow Shirt then announces they are moving on to Earth, and the Rocket accelerates, leaving Venus and the Sun behind as the scene transitions to a dark, star-filled space with Earth appearing in the distance, glowing with blue oceans and white clouds, signaling the next destination in their journey.

discovered_new_entities.Earth: A large, blue and white planet with visible continents, oceans, and cloud cover, shown in the distance against a dark, star-filled space. It appears as the next destination, glowing softly as the Rocket approaches.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars riding the Rocket through space, now approaching Earth, which is shown as a large, blue and white planet with visible continents and cloud cover. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that Earth is in the third position in the solar system, and that it takes 365 days for Earth to revolve around the Sun, with a '365 jours' sign appearing on screen. He then explains the seasons, stating that when the northern hemisphere is tilted toward the Sun, it is summer in the North and winter in the South, and vice versa. The Boy with Binoculars responds with interest, asking to learn about Mars. The Boy in Yellow Shirt agrees, and they accelerate the Rocket, leaving Earth behind. The scene transitions to Mars, a large, reddish-brown planet set against a vibrant nebula. The Boy in Yellow Shirt points out that Mars is the fourth planet in the solar system, and a sign appears indicating that a year on Mars lasts 687 days. The Rocket continues its journey toward Mars, with the two boys engaged in conversation as they travel through the star-filled expanse.

active_entities.Boy in Yellow Shirt: A boy with short brown hair, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown cargo pants, and dark sneakers. He acts as the guide, calmly explaining Earth’s position and orbit, gesturing as he speaks, and then announcing the transition to Mars with a confident tone.

discovered_new_entities.Mars: A large, reddish-brown planet with visible surface features, set against a colorful nebula. It is introduced as the fourth planet in the solar system, with a '687 jours' sign appearing to indicate the length of its year.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars seated on the Rocket, now approaching Mars, which is prominently displayed as a large, reddish-brown planet against a vibrant nebula. The Boy in Yellow Shirt gestures toward Mars, explaining that its year lasts 687 days, with on-screen text confirming this. He then points out that Mars' temperature varies between -140°C and 30°C, accompanied by a digital thermometer graphic. He explains that Mars is called 'the Red Planet' due to its soil being composed mainly of red iron oxide, which the Boy with Binoculars listens to with growing curiosity. After this explanation, the Boy in Yellow Shirt announces it’s time to head for Jupiter, and the Rocket begins to move away from Mars. The scene transitions to a view of Jupiter, a massive gas giant with distinct brown and white bands, as the Rocket approaches. The Boy in Yellow Shirt informs the viewer that Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system and is composed mostly of hydrogen and helium. He then asks about Jupiter’s revolution around the Sun, prompting the next segment of the journey. The background shifts from the colorful nebula near Mars to the dark, star-filled space surrounding Jupiter, emphasizing the vastness of space and the scale of the planets.

active_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers. He is seated on the Rocket, initially attentive as Mars is explained, then shows curiosity and engagement when the name 'Red Planet' is discussed, and finally looks forward with anticipation as they head toward Jupiter.

discovered_new_entities.Jupiter: A massive gas giant with distinct brown and white bands, visible against a dark, star-filled space. It is introduced as the largest planet in the solar system, with swirling cloud patterns and a prominent Great Red Spot, emphasizing its size and gaseous composition.
discovered_new_entities.Digital Thermometer Graphic: A white, handheld digital thermometer with a blue screen, appearing next to Mars to visually represent its temperature range of -140°C to 30°C, enhancing the educational content with a clear visual aid.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars seated on the Rocket, now floating in space near Jupiter, a massive gas giant with swirling brown and white bands. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that although Jupiter’s days are the shortest, its years are very long, taking about 12 Earth years to revolve around the Sun, as indicated by a floating '12 années' sign. The Boy with Binoculars reacts with surprise, and the Boy in Yellow Shirt suggests moving on to Saturn. The Rocket then accelerates, leaving Jupiter behind, and transitions to Saturn, a pale yellow planet with prominent, thick rings. The Boy in Yellow Shirt informs the viewer that Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun and the second largest in the solar system, famous for its beautiful ringlets. He then asks how long it takes Saturn to orbit the Sun, and the answer appears: '29,5 années', with a corresponding sign floating above. The Rocket remains near Saturn as the explanation concludes, with the vast, dark space and distant stars forming the backdrop.

active_entities.Boy with Binoculars: A young boy with spiky black hair, wearing a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light-colored sneakers. He is seated on the Rocket, initially attentive as Jupiter is explained, then shows surprise at its long year, and finally looks forward with curiosity as they approach Saturn.

It is introduced as the sixth planet from the Sun, the second largest in the solar system, and famous for its beautiful ringlets, with the rings clearly visible and rotating slowly.

discovered_new_entities.29,5 années Sign: A white, notepad-style sign with red clips, displaying '29,5 années' in bold yellow text, floating above the Rocket to visually represent Saturn’s orbital period.

segments.caption: The video continues the space journey with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars riding the Rocket through the cosmos. They approach Saturn, a pale yellow gas giant with prominent, multi-layered rings, set against a dark, star-filled space. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that Saturn takes 29.5 Earth years to orbit the Sun, with a floating '29,5 années' sign appearing above the Rocket to emphasize this. The Boy with Binoculars reacts with surprise, asking about Saturn’s temperature, to which the guide responds that it varies between -189°C and -139°C, causing the boy to shiver in reaction. The scene then transitions as they leave Saturn behind, with the Boy in Yellow Shirt announcing their next destination: Uranus. The Rocket accelerates, leaving Saturn’s rings in the distance, and approaches a large, pale blue planet—Uranus—set against a deep blue nebula. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that Uranus is considered an ice giant, composed of water, ammonia, and methane in solid form, and that it takes 84 years to orbit the Sun, a fact highlighted by a floating '84 ans' sign. He adds that this is the average human life expectancy on Earth, drawing a relatable comparison. The Boy with Binoculars listens attentively, showing curiosity and engagement throughout the educational journey.

discovered_new_entities.84 ans Sign: A white, notepad-style sign with red clips, displaying '84 ans' in bold yellow text, floating above the Rocket to visually represent Uranus’s orbital period.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars riding the Rocket through space, now approaching Uranus, a large pale blue planet. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that Uranus is an ice giant, and its temperature varies between -226°C and -197°C, as shown by a digital thermometer graphic appearing next to the planet. The Boy with Binoculars reacts with surprise, exclaiming it's too cold. The Rocket then accelerates, leaving Uranus behind as the Boy in Yellow Shirt announces their next destination: Neptune. The scene transitions to Neptune, a deep blue planet set against a vibrant nebula. The Boy in Yellow Shirt explains that Neptune is also an ice giant and that its parts could orbit at different speeds, which the Boy with Binoculars finds weird. A new digital thermometer graphic appears, showing Neptune’s temperature range of -218°C to -200°C. The Boy with Binoculars expresses gratitude for the information, and the Boy in Yellow Shirt responds with a simple 'No problem.' The Rocket continues its journey through the starry expanse, with the two boys seated calmly, observing the distant planets.

discovered_new_entities.Neptune: A large, deep blue planet with subtle cloud bands, set against a vibrant, colorful nebula. It is introduced as an ice giant, with a digital thermometer graphic appearing to show its temperature range of -218°C to -200°C.

segments.caption: The scene opens with the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars seated on a white and gray Rocket, flying through a star-filled space with a large blue planet visible in the background. The Boy in Yellow Shirt, wearing glasses, a yellow polo shirt, brown pants, and dark sneakers, speaks confidently, gesturing with his hand as he asks the Boy with Binoculars a question. The Boy with Binoculars, in a blue checkered shirt over a white t-shirt, dark pants, and light sneakers, listens attentively, then responds with a smile, correctly identifying Mars as 'The Red Planet.' The Boy in Yellow Shirt praises him, and the two continue their journey through space. The Rocket emits bright yellow flames as it moves forward. On-screen text appears in both French and English, including phrases like 'Il n'y a pas de quoi! No problem!', 'Maintenant je vais te poser une question, es-tu prêt? Now I'm gonna ask you a question, are you ready?', 'Quelle planète est surnommée « La planète Rouge ? Which planet is nicknamed "The Red Planet"?', 'Oui, je le sais! C'est la planète "Mars". Yes, I know! It is the planet "Mars".', and 'Très bien. Tu as tout à fait raison. Very well. You're absolutely right.' The video concludes with a call to action: 'Si vous avez aimé cette vidéo, appuyez sur le bouton « J'aime ». If you liked this video, hit the "Like" button.' followed by 'Et pour de nouvelles vidéos intéressantes, n'oubliez surtout pas de vous abonner à notre chaîne! And for new interesting videos, don't forget to subscribe to our channel!' and 'Merci de votre soutien! À la prochaine! Thank you for your support! See you next time!' The Rocket continues to fly through the dark, starry expanse, with the boys smiling and waving as the scene fades out.

It moves steadily through the starry background, with the flames becoming more pronounced as it propels forward, serving as their vehicle for the educational journey.

segments.caption: The video transitions from a dynamic space scene to a series of static comment screens, then concludes with a vibrant end card. Initially, the Rocket, carrying the Boy in Yellow Shirt and the Boy with Binoculars, speeds through a starry expanse, leaving a bright yellow trail, before exiting the frame. The scene then shifts to a bright yellow background displaying the word 'COMMENTS' in bold black letters, accompanied by five pink stars. A white comment box appears, showcasing viewer feedback in French, Portuguese, and Spanish, with visible likes, hearts, and reply options. The comments praise the video's educational value, storytelling, and pronunciation, with usernames like 'Wendy Hlatshwayo' and 'The Wondering Astronaut' highlighted. The comment box cycles through different sets of feedback, maintaining the same layout and branding. Finally, the video transitions to a soft pink gradient background with animated sparkles and a rotating green ring. The text 'THANKS FOR WATCHING!' appears in bold black letters at the top, with 'WATCH MORE' on either side and a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button in the center. Below, the phrase 'DON'T FORGET TO LIKE, COMMENT AND SUBSCRIBE!' encourages viewer engagement. The CUBE ACADEMY logo remains consistently in the top right corner throughout the segment.

entity_library.Mercury: A large, gray, cratered planet shown against a colorful nebula, with on-screen text indicating '88 jours' (88 days) to highlight its short year. It is visible at the start of the segment before the Rocket moves on.
entity_library.Venus: A large, glowing, rocky planet with a fiery orange and yellow surface, shown in close proximity to the Sun. It is described as Earth’s twin and highlighted for its extreme heat (460°C) and 225-day orbit.
entity_library.Sun: A massive, bright, orange-yellow star with visible solar flares and a glowing corona, dominating the background as the Rocket approaches Venus. It provides intense illumination and a warm, radiant atmosphere for the scene.
entity_library.Earth: A large, blue and white planet with visible continents, oceans, and cloud cover, shown in the distance against a dark, star-filled space. It appears as the next destination, glowing softly as the Rocket approaches and then recedes into the background.
entity_library.Mars: A large, reddish-brown planet with visible surface features, set against a colorful nebula. It is introduced as the fourth planet in the solar system, with a '687 jours' sign appearing to indicate the length of its year, and later shown with a digital thermometer graphic to illustrate its temperature range.
entity_library.Jupiter: A massive gas giant with distinct brown and white bands, visible against a dark, star-filled space. It is introduced as having the shortest days but longest years, with swirling cloud patterns and a prominent Great Red Spot, emphasizing its size and gaseous composition.
entity_library.Digital Thermometer Graphic: A white, handheld digital thermometer with a blue screen, appearing next to Uranus and later Neptune to visually represent their respective temperature ranges, enhancing the educational content with a clear visual aid.
entity_library.Saturn: A pale yellow gas giant with prominent, thick, multi-layered rings, visible against a dark, star-filled space. It is introduced as the sixth planet from the Sun, the second largest in the solar system, and famous for its beautiful ringlets, with the rings clearly visible and rotating slowly.
entity_library.12 années Sign: A white, notepad-style sign with red clips, displaying '12 années' in bold yellow text, floating above the Rocket to visually represent Jupiter’s orbital period.
entity_library.29,5 années Sign: A white, notepad-style sign with red clips, displaying '29,5 années' in bold yellow text, floating above the Rocket to visually represent Saturn’s orbital period.
entity_library.Uranus: A large, pale blue planet with a smooth, icy surface, set against a deep blue nebula. It is introduced as an ice giant, with a digital thermometer graphic appearing to show its temperature range of -226°C to -197°C.
entity_library.84 ans Sign: A white, notepad-style sign with red clips, displaying '84 ans' in bold yellow text, floating above the Rocket to visually represent Uranus’s orbital period.
entity_library.Neptune: A large, deep blue planet with subtle cloud bands, set against a vibrant, colorful nebula. It is introduced as an ice giant, with a digital thermometer graphic appearing to show its temperature range of -218°C to -200°C.
entity_library.CUBE ACADEMY Logo: A yellow and blue logo in the top right corner, reading 'CUBE ACADEMY' in bold, stylized text, remaining consistently visible throughout the segment as the channel's branding.
entity_library.Comments Section: A white rectangular box on a yellow background displaying viewer comments in multiple languages, with usernames, timestamps, likes, hearts, and reply options, cycling through different sets of feedback.
entity_library.End Card: A soft pink gradient background with animated sparkles and a rotating green ring, featuring bold black text 'THANKS FOR WATCHING!', 'WATCH MORE' on both sides, a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button, and the phrase 'DON'T FORGET TO LIKE, COMMENT AND SUBSCRIBE!' below.
